ogTitle: Hoddys Well vs Holleton: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hoddys Well and Holleton.
heading: Hoddys Well vs Holleton — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hoddys Well, Western Australia and Holleton, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Holleton has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Hoddys Well (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hoddys Well or Holleton safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hoddys Well has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Holleton scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hoddys Well scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Holleton scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hoddys Well or Holleton?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Holleton has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
